---
title: "Neurologic Examination"
---

THE NEUROLOGICAL EXAMINATION
Mental Status
Arousal state: Alert, Sleepy, Lethargic, Stuporous, Coma. Best to describe (e.g. opens eyes spontaneously vs
tovoice vs sternal rub vs deep pain)
Attention: spell WORLD backwards, months backwards, serial 7’s, count from 20 to 1
Language: Fluency, repetition, naming, reading, writing, comprehension (1-step midline vs appendicular
commands vs 2-step vs 2-step reversed grammar (touch your nose after pointing to the ceiling)
Fund of Knowledge- current events, quality of medical Hx
Praxis: Show me how you use a hammer
Memory: 3-5 objects at 1 minute, digit span (avg 7), story retelling. Distant memory.
Visuospatial: Organization of space (clock drawing, complex figure copy)
Neglect- extinction, line bisection, target elimination
Executive: Organization of knowledge, inhibition, sequencing, perseveration
Verbal fluency: name animals in 1 minute (>20 is nml)
Oral trails (A1, B2, 3C....), Luria 3-step (fist, edge, palm) registration and repeat 3 times.
Go/no go (e.g. raise your hand every time I say a letter, except when I say ‘D’)
Cranial Nerves
Fundus—disc/vasculature/venous pulsations/retina
Visual acuity (pinhole to correct refraction), color vision (especially red desaturation)
Visual fields (cut/neglect)
Pupils- size, reactivity, ptosis
Extraocular mm- saccades and smooth pursuit (ductions-both eyes, versions-one eye, alignment)
Face sensation—LT/PP/temp, v1-v3 dist, corneal reflex
Face strength— central vs peripheral, hyperacusis, dysgusia, corneal dehydration
Hearing— hi/lo pitch, Weber, Rinne. Finger rub is usually enough.
Vestibular- VOR, past pointing, Fukuda march test, Dix-Hallpike, nystagmus
Palate elevation—Aaaah, gag, uvula position, myoclonus (CTT lesion)
SCM strength/bulk—innervates ipsi SCM, contra trap
Tongue—position, bulk, fasciculations, strength (tongue against cheek)
Motor
Bulk (atrophy, fasciculations)
Tone (flaccid, spastic/clasp knife, rigid/lead pipe), abnl mvmt (freq, amp, rest, action, tremor)
Strength: Drift, satelliting, isolated muscles (3=antigravity),
functional tests- heel/toe walking, rise from chair with arms crossed, squat, deep knee bend
Sensory
Extremities/trunk/back/perineum. Look for a sensory level; Romberg
Anterolateral system (temp/pp/some ft)
Dorsal Columns (lt/vib/discrimination/proprioception/some ft)
Cortical sensory signs- graphasthesia, extinction, stereoagnosia
Coordination
Midline/vermis: trunk/hemispheres: appendicular coordination
FTN, HTS, titubation, optic ataxia, nystagmus, rhythm testing (RAM)
Reflexes
DTRs: 0 to 4+: 3= spread of reflex, not amplitude of response, Jaw jerk (to distinguish cord/brain)
Frontal release (glabellar, rooting, snouting, palmomental, grasp)
Hoffman/Babinski, clonus
Gait
Base of stance, Stability of stance (provocation), posture, Initiation of gait, Stepping, Base of gait, Arm swing,
Turning strength and balance–tandem walk
